# web/data/generated/

**Phase 2B test output.** Files here are generated by `scripts/vault-to-json.js`.

```
node scripts/vault-to-json.js
```

## What this is

These JSON files are produced by parsing YAML frontmatter from `vault/Places/`,
`vault/Characters/`, `vault/Events/`, and `vault/Stories/`. They use the same
field structure as the hand-authored files in `web/data/` (parent directory).

## Current status

- `places.json` — from vault/Places/*.md
- `characters.json` — from vault/Characters/*.md
- `events.json` — from vault/Events/*.md
- `stories.json` — from vault/Stories/*.md

The web engine (`web/engine.js`) still loads from `web/data/*.json` (the
hand-authored Phase 1 files). This generated/ directory is a staging area.

## Phase 2C: promoting to live data

When the vault has enough entries to replace the hand-authored JSON:
1. Verify generated output matches expected structure
2. In `web/engine.js`, change the four `fetch('./data/X.json')` calls to
   `fetch('./data/generated/X.json')` (or copy generated files to parent)
3. Test the preview — all markers, panel content, and chronology should work
4. Remove hand-authored JSON or archive it to `web/data/archive/`

## Field contract

See `docs/OBSIDIAN_DATA_CONTRACT.md` for the canonical list of which frontmatter
fields are exported to JSON and which are vault-only.
